name: rust-pro
description: Master Rust 1.75+ with modern async patterns, advanced type system
capabilities: Design and implement async web services with Tokio runtime
Solve ownership, lifetime, and borrow checker issues
Implement safe FFI wrappers around C libraries
Optimize memory usage and cache locality
Create custom error handling with thiserror and anyhow
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files and detected 0 potential security issues. This is a Rust developer persona definition that provides guidance on Rust programming without any network, filesystem, or system access capabilities. Risk score: 0/100.
valueStatement: Build high-performance, memory-safe Rust applications with expert guidance on async patterns, advanced type system features, and production-ready systems programming.
limitations: Cannot execute or compile Rust code directly
Cannot access local file system or project files
Cannot run benchmarks or profiling tools
Provides guidance only, not runtime execution

useCases.title: Building Async Web Services
useCases.description: Design and implement high-performance web APIs using axum, Tokio, and async patterns with proper error handling and backpressure

useCases.title: Performance Optimization
useCases.description: Analyze and optimize Rust code for memory usage, cache efficiency, and zero-cost abstractions while maintaining safety

useCases.targetUser: Developers integrating Rust with native libraries
useCases.title: FFI and Unsafe Code
useCases.description: Create safe wrappers around C libraries with proper safety invariants and documentation for unsafe code blocks

promptTemplates.title: Basic Rust Question
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain how [Rust concept] works in Rust 1.75+ and provide a code example.
promptTemplates.scenario: Learning a specific Rust feature or syntax

promptTemplates.title: Debug Ownership Issues
promptTemplates.prompt: I have this Rust code that fails with a borrow checker error. Analyze the ownership model and suggest a fix: [code]

promptTemplates.title: Design Async Service
promptTemplates.prompt: Design an async web service in Rust that handles [use case] with Tokio. Include error handling, proper shutdown, and testing approach.

promptTemplates.title: Optimize Performance
promptTemplates.prompt: Profile and optimize this Rust code for [metric]. Suggest zero-cost abstractions, memory layout improvements, or algorithm changes: [code]
promptTemplates.scenario: Improving runtime performance of Rust applications

bestPractices: Prefer Result types over exceptions for error handling
Use Arc and Rc only when shared ownership is truly needed
Document all unsafe code blocks with explicit safety invariants
antiPatterns: Using unwrap() in production code without proper error handling
Ignoring the borrow checker by using unsafe instead of finding a safe solution
Blocking async code with synchronous I/O operations

faq.question: What Rust version does this skill cover?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on Rust 1.75+ features including const generics, improved type inference, and async improvements.

faq.question: Can this skill write and compile code for me?
faq.answer: This skill provides guidance, examples, and code suggestions but cannot execute or compile Rust code directly.

faq.question: Does this skill support Tokio and async programming?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill has extensive knowledge of Tokio ecosystem including axum, tower, hyper, and async patterns.

faq.question: Can this help with FFI and unsafe code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill covers safe abstractions over unsafe code, FFI patterns, and proper safety documentation.

faq.question: What testing frameworks does this skill recommend?
faq.answer: It covers unit tests, property-based testing with proptest, benchmarking with criterion.rs, and mocking with mockall.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for beginners?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for intermediate to advanced Rust developers. Beginners should start with basic Rust tutorials first.
